name.zh: 首個 Token 回應時間

summary.zh: TTFT 是從請求送出到模型開始輸出第一個 token 所需的時間。
summary.en: A metric used to evaluate AI service quality, reliability, answer quality, or user experience.

explanation.zh:
TTFT（首個 token 回應時間）是 Time to First Token（首個 token 回應時間），指從請求送出到模型開始輸出第一個 token（語詞單位）的時間。

為什麼重要：許多聊天介面採用串流輸出，使用者看到第一個字出現時會覺得系統有反應。即使完整回答需要較久，只要 TTFT 低，體驗通常較好。
explanation.en:
TTFT, or time to first token, measures how long it takes before the first generated token is returned.

This metric should be interpreted together with other product and system indicators. A single number can hide important failure patterns, so teams should segment results by workflow, user type, traffic level, and error category when possible.

For AI products, this should be evaluated with real workflows rather than isolated demo prompts. It is most useful when connected to user impact and operational decisions.

pm_notes.zh:
TTFT衡量的是「開始回應」的速度，不等於完整任務完成時間。若 TTFT 高，使用者容易覺得系統卡住。

可採取行動：聊天型產品應把 TTFT 納入體驗指標，並評估 streaming、模型選型與前處理時間。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should track TTFT for chat-like experiences. It strongly affects whether users feel the system is responsive.

Set acceptance thresholds and monitor the metric after launch, especially for user-facing or workflow-critical features. Set an acceptance threshold and monitor it after launch, especially for user-facing or workflow-critical features.

primary_sources.title: Microsoft Learn - Azure OpenAI performance and latency
OpenAI API - Realtime and audio

name.zh: 最後 Token 完成時間

summary.zh: TTLT 是從請求送出到模型完整輸出最後一個 token 所需的時間。
summary.en: A metric used to evaluate AI service quality, reliability, answer quality, or user experience.

explanation.zh:
TTLT（最後 token 完成時間）是 Time to Last Token（最後 token 完成時間），指模型完整輸出最後一個 token（語詞單位）的時間，也就是完整回答完成時間。

管理方式：限制 output token（輸出 token）、簡化格式、使用較快模型、拆分任務或使用 async processing。
explanation.en:
TTLT, or time to last token, measures how long it takes for the full generated response to finish.

This metric should be interpreted together with other product and system indicators. A single number can hide important failure patterns, so teams should segment results by workflow, user type, traffic level, and error category when possible.

For AI products, this should be evaluated with real workflows rather than isolated demo prompts. It is most useful when connected to user impact and operational decisions.

pm_notes.zh:
回答越長，TTLT通常越高。若業務場景需要完整結果才能繼續下一步，例如產生 JSON 給系統解析，TTLT 比 TTFT更重要。

可採取行動：對需要完整結果才能進下一步的流程，設定 TTLT 目標並控制輸出長度。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should track TTLT when full answer completion matters, such as reports, summaries, or structured outputs.

Set acceptance thresholds and monitor the metric after launch, especially for user-facing or workflow-critical features. Set an acceptance threshold and monitor it after launch, especially for user-facing or workflow-critical features.

primary_sources.title: Microsoft Learn - Azure OpenAI performance and latency
OpenAI API - Realtime and audio

summary.zh: Uptime 是系統持續正常運行的時間，通常與 availability 一起衡量。
summary.en: A metric used to evaluate AI service quality, reliability, answer quality, or user experience.

explanation.zh:
Uptime 是系統持續正常運行的時間，通常與 availability 一起衡量。

Uptime 只能表示服務是否運行，不能代表回答品質，因此需搭配 error rate、latency（延遲時間）與品質指標。
explanation.en:
Uptime is the amount or percentage of time a service is available and running. It is commonly used as a reliability indicator.

For AI products, uptime must include the full user-facing workflow, not only the model API. Retrieval services, tool integrations, authentication, and frontend systems can also affect whether the feature is usable.

For AI products, this should be evaluated with real workflows rather than isolated demo prompts. It is most useful when connected to user impact and operational decisions.

pm_notes.zh:
PoC（概念驗證）階段可能不需要嚴格 uptime，但正式上線後，AI 功能若成為業務流程的一部分，就需要監控 uptime、設定告警與準備故障處理流程。

可採取行動：正式上線後設定 uptime 監控與告警，並定期檢查服務中斷對業務流程的影響。
pm_notes.en: PMs should define which components count toward uptime for the user experience. If the model is available but retrieval or tools are down, the AI feature may still be effectively unavailable. Set an acceptance threshold and monitor it after launch, especially for user-facing or workflow-critical features.

primary_sources.title: Google SRE Book - Service Level Objectives
AWS - What is a Service Level Agreement?

summary.zh: Batch size 是模型每次訓練更新時使用多少筆資料。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Batch size 是模型每次訓練更新時使用多少筆資料。

技術意義：較大的 batch size 可能提高訓練效率，但需要更多 GPU（圖形處理器）記憶體；較小 batch size 記憶體壓力低，但訓練可能較不穩定或較慢。
explanation.en:
Batch size is the number of training examples processed before the model updates its weights. It affects training stability, speed, memory usage, and compute requirements.

Larger batches may use hardware more efficiently but require more memory. Smaller batches may be easier to run but can make training noisier or slower.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: Batch size 會影響訓練成本、速度與模型效果。若 GPU VRAM不足，技術團隊可能需要調整 batch size、使用梯度累積或更小模型。
pm_notes.en: PMs should understand batch size as a training-resource factor. If training is slow, expensive, or unstable, ask the team how batch size and hardware constraints affect the plan. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Checkpoint 是訓練過程中保存下來的模型狀態。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Checkpoint 是訓練過程中保存下來的模型狀態。

用途包括：訓練中斷後恢復、比較不同訓練階段效果、選擇最佳模型版本、回滾到較穩定狀態。
explanation.en:
A checkpoint is a saved state of a model during or after training. It allows teams to resume training, compare versions, or roll back to a better-performing model.

Checkpoints are useful for experimentation because the best model may not be the final one trained. Teams often evaluate multiple checkpoints before selecting one for deployment.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: Checkpoint 會占用儲存空間，也涉及模型資產管理與權限控管。正式專案應明確保存策略與命名規則。
pm_notes.en: PMs should ask which checkpoint is being promoted and why. The selected version should be tied to evaluation results, not simply the latest training run. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer
OpenAI Developers - Fine-tuning

summary.zh: Dataset 是訓練、微調或評估模型使用的資料集合，可包含輸入、標準答案、標籤、偏好資料、metadata 與切分資訊。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Dataset 是訓練、微調或評估模型使用的資料集合，可包含輸入、標準答案、標籤、偏好資料、metadata 與切分資訊。

常見拆分：training data、validation data、test data。三者應避免資料洩漏，才能正確評估模型泛化能力。
explanation.en:
A dataset is a collection of examples used for training, validation, testing, evaluation, or analysis.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
資料集需要考慮來源、品質、代表性、隱私、授權、標註一致性與資料切分。資料品質通常比模型選型更影響最終效果。

可採取行動：建立資料集資料卡，記錄來源、授權、時間範圍、敏感性、切分方式與適用任務。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should clarify the dataset purpose, source, ownership, quality, and whether sensitive data is included.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Datasets documentation
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Distillation 是用大型模型教小型模型，使小模型學會接近大模型的行為。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Distillation 是用大型模型教小型模型，使小模型學會接近大模型的行為。

應用方式：先用大模型產生高品質訓練資料或示範，再訓練較小模型服務特定任務。
explanation.en:
Distillation trains a smaller model to imitate a larger model’s behavior. It can reduce cost and latency while preserving some quality.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 蒸餾可降低成本與 latency，適合高頻、任務固定、可接受部分能力折衷的場景。但小模型可能犧牲泛化能力、推理能力或長尾問題表現。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should evaluate whether the smaller model is good enough for the target workflow, not only cheaper.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI Developers - Fine-tuning
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: DPO 是 Direct Preference Optimization，一種使用偏好資料調整模型的方法，流程通常比傳統 RLHF（人類回饋強化學習）更簡化。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
DPO 是 Direct Preference Optimization，一種使用偏好資料調整模型的方法，流程通常比傳統 RLHF（人類回饋強化學習）更簡化。

它需要成對偏好資料，例如同一輸入下「較佳回答」與「較差回答」，讓模型直接學習偏好的輸出行為。
explanation.en:
DPO, or direct preference optimization, is a training method that uses preference pairs to make a model favor better responses over worse ones.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: DPO 仍依賴高品質偏好標註。若偏好標準不一致，模型可能學到錯誤風格或過度迎合某些標註偏好。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should ask how preference data is collected and whether it reflects real product expectations.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face TRL - DPO Trainer
Hugging Face Blog - RLHF

entry_metadata.change_note: 發布前中文內容可讀性修正

summary.zh: Epoch 是模型完整看過一次訓練資料的過程。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Epoch 是模型完整看過一次訓練資料的過程。例如訓練資料有 10,000 筆，模型完整學完這 10,000 筆一次，就是 1 epoch（訓練輪次）。

討論訓練計畫時，epoch 是估算訓練時間與成本的重要參數之一。
explanation.en:
An epoch is one complete pass through the training dataset. If a model trains for multiple epochs, it sees the same training examples multiple times.

More epochs can help a model learn the task better, but too many can increase cost and cause overfitting, where the model performs well on training data but poorly on new examples.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: Epoch 太少可能學不夠；太多可能 overfitting，也會增加訓練成本與時間。最佳 epoch數通常要透過 validation data 觀察。
pm_notes.en: PMs do not need to set epoch counts directly, but should ask whether training performance and test performance are both improving. A model that only improves on training data may not generalize well. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

entry_metadata.change_note: 發布前中文內容可讀性修正

summary.zh: Fine-tuning 是在既有模型基礎上，用特定資料進一步訓練，使模型更適合特定任務、語氣或輸出格式。
summary.en: Further training an existing model on task-specific examples to shape behavior or output format.

explanation.zh:
Fine-tuning 是在既有模型基礎上，用特定資料進一步訓練，使模型更適合特定任務、語氣或輸出格式。

適合情境：穩定輸出特定格式、特定分類任務、特定寫作風格、任務模式固定且有大量高品質標註資料。不適合情境：只是要模型知道最新公司規章，通常 RAG（檢索增強生成）更適合。
explanation.en:
Fine-tuning adapts an existing model using additional training data so it behaves better for a specific task, style, format, or domain.

It is useful when the desired behavior is repetitive and can be represented with high-quality examples. It is not usually the best way to update factual knowledge that changes frequently; RAG is often better for that.

Fine-tuning adds cost and maintenance, so it should be justified by measurable improvements over prompting, RAG, or tool use.

pm_notes.zh:
Fine-tuning 不是把公司所有知識塞進模型。它較適合調整模型行為與輸出模式，知識更新通常應透過知識庫與 RAG管理。

可採取行動：先確認問題是否可用 prompt或 RAG 解決；若仍需微調，要求提供資料量、評估方式、成本與維護計畫。
pm_notes.en: PMs should first ask whether the problem can be solved with prompt design, RAG, or workflow changes. If fine-tuning is proposed, require clear training data, evaluation criteria, baseline comparison, and maintenance plan. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: IBM Think - What is fine-tuning?
OpenAI Developers - Fine-tuning

summary.zh: Ground truth 是用來評估模型答案是否正確的標準參考答案或標準判斷。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Ground truth 是用來評估模型答案是否正確的標準參考答案或標準判斷。

好的 ground truth 能讓評估更客觀，也能協助團隊比較不同模型、prompt（提示詞）或 RAG（檢索增強生成）策略。
explanation.en:
Ground truth is the reference answer or label used to evaluate a model or system.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
GenAI 很多任務沒有唯一標準答案，因此 ground truth 可能不是單一文字答案，而是評分 rubric、必要資訊清單、禁止錯誤清單、來源依據與人工審核規則。

可採取行動：對無唯一答案的任務建立評分 rubric，明確定義必要資訊、禁止錯誤、可接受變體、審核者角色與更新頻率，避免標準答案過期或不同審核者判斷不一致。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should define ground truth carefully. For open-ended GenAI tasks, a rubric may be more useful than one fixed answer.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary
MLflow - LLM Evaluation and Agent Evaluation

summary.zh: Hyperparameter 是訓練前設定的參數，例如 learning rate、batch size、epoch 與 dropout。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Hyperparameter 是訓練前設定、不是模型自己學出來的參數，例如 learning rate、batch size、epoch（訓練輪次）、weight decay、dropout。

在專案管理上，hyperparameter（超參數） tuning 代表需要預留實驗時間、算力成本與評估流程。
explanation.en:
A hyperparameter is a setting chosen before or during training, such as learning rate, batch size, or number of epochs.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
超參數會影響訓練成本與結果，需要透過實驗調整。每次實驗都應記錄設定與結果，否則無法比較或回溯。

可採取行動：要求訓練實驗記錄主要超參數、資料版本與評估結果，避免無法比較不同實驗。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should track hyperparameters for experiments that influence launch decisions. They affect quality, cost, and reproducibility.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

summary.zh: Labeling 是為資料加上標準答案、分類、偏好或評分的過程。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Labeling 是為資料加上標準答案、分類、偏好或評分的過程。

範例：將使用者問題標註為產品功能、帳號問題、付款問題、技術支援或意見回饋；為模型回答標註正確、部分正確、錯誤；比較兩個回答哪個較好。
explanation.en:
Labeling is the process of adding categories, scores, answers, or annotations to data.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
標註規則必須清楚，否則不同標註人員可能產生不一致資料。需要設計標註指南、抽樣複核與一致性檢查。

可採取行動：建立標註指南與抽樣複核流程，確保不同標註人員對同一任務有一致判斷。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should ensure label guidelines are clear. Inconsistent labels create inconsistent model behavior.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Datasets documentation
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Learning rate 控制模型每次更新參數的幅度。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Learning rate 控制模型每次更新參數的幅度。

簡單理解：學習率太高，模型可能學過頭、震盪或不穩；學習率太低，模型學得太慢，訓練時間變長，也可能卡在不佳結果。
explanation.en:
Learning rate controls how much model weights change during each training update. If it is too high, training may become unstable; if it is too low, training may be slow or fail to improve enough.

PMs usually do not tune learning rate directly, but it affects training cost, experiment timelines, and final model quality. It is most relevant when a project involves training or fine-tuning rather than only prompting or RAG.

pm_notes.zh:
Learning rate 是重要超參數，調整會影響訓練品質與成本。訓練失敗不一定是模型或資料問題，也可能是 learning rate 不合適。

可採取行動：若訓練效果不穩或 loss異常，要求團隊檢查 learning rate 與訓練曲線，而不是只更換模型。
pm_notes.en: PMs do not need to choose the learning rate, but should understand that unstable or slow training can affect timeline and quality. Ask for training results and evaluation evidence before accepting a tuned model. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: LoRA 是 Low-Rank Adaptation，一種較省資源的模型微調方法。
summary.en: A parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that trains small adapter weights instead of the full model.

explanation.zh:
LoRA 是 Low-Rank Adaptation，一種較省資源的模型微調方法。它不直接更新整個模型，而是在模型上加入小型可訓練模組。

優點：訓練成本較低、需要較少 GPU（圖形處理器）記憶體、可為不同任務保存不同 adapter、部署與切換較彈性。
explanation.en:
LoRA is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that trains small adapter weights instead of updating the full model.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
LoRA 適合資源有限但需要任務客製化的情境。不過仍需評估品質、維運複雜度與 adapter 管理。

可採取行動：若採 LoRA，需定義 adapter 命名、版本、適用任務、合併/載入方式與權限管理。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should consider LoRA when customization is needed but full fine-tuning is too costly or operationally heavy.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

summary.zh: Loss 是模型預測與標準答案之間的差距。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Loss 是模型預測與標準答案之間的差距。訓練目標通常是讓 loss（損失值）下降。

應搭配 validation metrics、test metrics、人工評估與業務 KPI 判斷。
explanation.en:
Loss is a training signal that measures how far the model output is from the expected answer.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
Loss 下降不代表業務效果一定好。模型可能在訓練資料上 loss很低，但真實任務表現差，或回答格式、合規性、可讀性仍不符合要求。

可採取行動：評估時不要只看 loss，還要看實際任務指標、人工審查、格式穩定性與安全表現。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should not rely on loss alone. A lower loss does not always mean better product behavior.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

summary.zh: Max tokens 限制模型最多可以輸出多少 token（語詞單位），是控制成本、回答長度與 latency（延遲時間）的重要參數。
summary.en: A setting that caps how many tokens the model can generate in its response.

explanation.zh:
Max tokens 限制模型最多可以輸出多少 token（語詞單位），是控制成本、回答長度與 latency（延遲時間）的重要參數。

用途包括：控制生成成本、避免回答過長、降低等待時間、確保輸出符合 UI 或下游系統限制。
explanation.en:
Max tokens is a setting that limits how much text the model can generate in a response. It is commonly used to control output length, cost, and latency.

If the limit is too low, the response may be cut off before the task is complete. If it is too high, the system may produce unnecessarily long answers and increase cost.

The right value depends on the use case, such as short support replies, summaries, structured extraction, or long-form drafting.

pm_notes.zh:
設太小可能導致回答被截斷；設太大可能增加成本與等待時間。應依任務設定不同上限，例如客服短答、文件摘要、JSON 抽取可有不同 max tokens。

可採取行動：為每個 use case 設定合理 max tokens，並在 UAT（使用者驗收測試）檢查是否發生回答截斷或成本過高。
pm_notes.en: PMs should define reasonable output lengths by use case and test whether max-token settings cause truncation. Use the setting to manage cost and latency, but do not set it so low that it breaks task completion. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API Reference - Chat completions parameters

summary.zh: Model parameter 可指模型內部權重，也可指呼叫模型 API 時設定的推論參數。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Model parameter 在 GenAI 討論中可能有兩種意思：一是模型內部訓練出的權重參數，例如 7B、70B；二是使用模型 API（應用程式介面）時可調整的推論參數，例如 temperature、top_p、max tokens。

建議在文件或需求中明確寫出 model version（模型版本）、inference parameters 與實驗結果，避免溝通混淆。
explanation.en:
A model parameter is an internal value learned during training. In large models, parameter count is often used as a rough size indicator.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 專案討論時要分清楚「模型規模參數」和「模型使用設定」。前者影響模型能力、部署成本與硬體需求；後者影響輸出風格、穩定性、成本與延遲。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should not equate more parameters with a better product. Evaluate quality, cost, latency, and deployment constraints.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API - Models
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

entry_metadata.change_note: 發布前中文內容可讀性修正

summary.zh: Model version 是使用的模型具體版本。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Model version 是使用的模型具體版本。不同版本即使名稱相近，能力、成本、速度、上下文長度與回答風格都可能不同。

建議在日誌中保存 model name、version、prompt（提示詞） version、參數與回應結果，方便追查品質變化。
explanation.en:
Model version identifies the specific model release used by an application. Different versions may vary in quality, speed, cost, safety behavior, context length, and output style.

Even when the model name looks similar, a version change can affect product behavior. This matters for regression testing, cost forecasting, and user support.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
模型版本更新可能造成產品行為改變，因此正式服務應記錄模型版本，切換前做回歸測試，也就是用既有測試題重新確認結果沒有被改壞，並準備回復方案。

可採取行動：模型版本升級前，應請專案團隊執行回歸測試，也就是用既有測試題重新確認品質、格式與安全規則沒有被改壞，並記錄切換日期、版本差異與回復方案。
pm_notes.en: PMs should track the model version used in each environment. Before upgrading, run regression tests, meaning existing test cases are reused to check that previous behavior has not been broken. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API - Models
OpenAI API - Prompt engineering

summary.zh: Model weight 是模型訓練後學到的內部數值參數，決定模型如何處理輸入並產生輸出。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Model weight 是模型訓練後學到的內部數值參數，決定模型如何處理輸入並產生輸出。

權重版本、授權、儲存位置、存取權限與部署方式都應納入治理。
explanation.en:
Model weights are the learned values that determine how a model transforms inputs into outputs.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 權重是模型核心資產。開源模型通常開放權重或部分權重；閉源模型則不公開。若企業自行微調，微調後權重或 adapter 也需要管理與保護。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should understand that model weights are a core asset. Access, licensing, and deployment rights matter for open and private models.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary
Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer

summary.zh: Overfitting 是模型太熟悉訓練資料，導致在訓練資料表現很好，但遇到新資料表現變差。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Overfitting 是模型太熟悉訓練資料，導致在訓練資料表現很好，但遇到新資料表現變差。

防範方式：使用獨立測試集、交叉驗證、早停、資料擴充與更合理的訓練設定。
explanation.en:
Overfitting happens when a model performs well on training data but poorly on new examples.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
若模型在 training data 表現很好，但 validation/test 或真實使用表現差，可能是過度擬合。常見原因包括訓練資料太少、epoch太多、資料重複或模型太大。

可採取行動：要求團隊比較 training、validation、test 與真實案例表現，確認模型是否只記住訓練資料。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should compare training, validation, and real-world performance. Strong training results alone are not enough.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

summary.zh: Pretraining 是模型在大量通用資料上進行初始訓練，建立基礎語言能力、知識與表示能力。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Pretraining 是模型在大量通用資料上進行初始訓練，建立基礎語言能力、知識與表示能力。

簡單理解：這是模型學會語言、常識與基本模式的階段，通常需要極大的資料量與運算資源。
explanation.en:
Pretraining is the initial large-scale training stage that gives a model broad capabilities before task-specific adaptation.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 企業一般不會自行做 foundation model pretraining，因為成本極高。企業更常做的是選模型、建 RAG、微調或部署。
pm_notes.en:
PMs rarely manage pretraining directly, but should know it is very expensive and different from fine-tuning or RAG.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: IBM Think - What are foundation models?

summary.zh: RLHF 是 Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback，透過人類偏好回饋調整模型，使回答更符合人類期待。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
RLHF 是 Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback，透過人類偏好回饋調整模型，使回答更符合人類期待。

簡單理解：人類比較多個回答，標示哪個較好，系統訓練 reward model 或直接用偏好訊號，讓模型更傾向產生被人類偏好的回答。
explanation.en:
RLHF, or reinforcement learning from human feedback, uses human preferences to make model outputs more helpful or aligned.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: RLHF 成本高、流程複雜，企業一般專案較少自行執行，但需要理解大型模型為何能更會對話、更遵循指令。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should understand RLHF as a model-improvement method, but most product teams will use models that already went through this process.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

summary.zh: Seed 是隨機種子，用來控制生成隨機性，使相同輸入在某些條件下較可能產生相同輸出。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Seed 是隨機種子，用來控制生成隨機性，使相同輸入在某些條件下較可能產生相同輸出。

適用情境：測試、回歸驗證、demo 穩定輸出，但正式品質仍應靠評估與監控，而不是只靠 seed。
explanation.en:
A seed is a value used to make random generation more repeatable in systems that support it.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 不是所有模型或 API（應用程式介面）都支援 seed。即使支援，也不一定保證完全可重現，因為模型版本、系統更新、硬體推論環境與供應商設定都可能影響結果。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should not assume perfect reproducibility. Model version, provider behavior, and infrastructure can still affect outputs.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API - Text generation
OpenAI API - Models

summary.zh: SFT 是 Supervised Fine-tuning，使用「輸入與理想答案」配對資料來微調模型。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
SFT 是 Supervised Fine-tuning，使用「輸入與理想答案」配對資料來微調模型。常見目標是讓模型更穩定地遵循特定格式、語氣、任務流程或領域作答方式。

範例：輸入是使用者問題，理想答案是符合政策、格式一致且可採用的標準回覆。模型透過這些範例學會在類似情境下輸出期望答案。SFT 較適合調整行為與輸出模式，不適合作為快速更新企業知識的主要方式；知識更新通常更適合透過 RAG 或資料管線管理。
explanation.en:
SFT, or supervised fine-tuning, trains a model on examples that pair an input with an ideal output. It teaches the model to follow a desired behavior, format, tone, or task pattern.

SFT is useful when teams have high-quality examples of what good output looks like. It is less suitable when the main problem is frequently changing factual knowledge, where RAG may be easier to maintain.

pm_notes.zh:
SFT 品質高度依賴訓練資料品質。若標準答案不一致、標註規則不清楚或資料覆蓋不足，模型也會學到不穩定或錯誤的行為。

可採取行動：在啟動 SFT 前先確認標準答案品質、標註一致性、測試集與評估方式，並釐清需求是行為調整、格式穩定，還是知識更新。
pm_notes.en: PMs should ensure SFT examples are high quality, consistent, and representative of real tasks. Ask how the fine-tuned model performs against a baseline model using the same evaluation set. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer
OpenAI Developers - Fine-tuning

summary.zh: Stop sequence 是指定模型遇到某些文字或符號時停止生成，用於控制輸出邊界。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Stop sequence 是指定模型遇到某些文字或符號時停止生成，用於控制輸出邊界。

應用場景：固定格式輸出、多段生成任務、避免模型繼續生成不需要內容、在解析多筆資料時標示結束點。
explanation.en:
A stop sequence tells the model when to stop generating, such as when a delimiter or marker appears.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
Stop sequence 是格式穩定性的輔助工具，但若設計不當，可能造成回答提早截斷或漏掉重要內容。

可採取行動：測試 stop sequence 是否造成提早截斷，特別是 JSON、表格或多段輸出場景。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should use stop sequences when output boundaries matter, especially for structured formats or multi-part generation.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API Reference - Chat completions parameters
OpenAI Help - Controlling the length of OpenAI model responses

summary.zh: Temperature 是控制模型回答隨機性的推論參數。
summary.en: An inference parameter that controls how random or creative model outputs can be.

explanation.zh:
Temperature 是控制模型回答隨機性的推論參數。低 temperature 讓回答較穩定、保守、可預期；高 temperature 讓回答較多變、創意較高。

適用情境：客服、法規摘要、文件問答、資料整理通常使用低 temperature；行銷文案、腦力激盪、創意發想可使用較高 temperature。
explanation.en:
Temperature is an inference setting that controls how much randomness the model uses when generating text.

Lower values make outputs more stable and predictable. Higher values produce more variation, which can help with brainstorming or creative writing but may be risky for factual, structured, or policy-bound tasks.

Temperature does not make the model more knowledgeable; it changes how deterministic or varied the output is.

pm_notes.zh:
正式業務場景通常偏向低 temperature，以確保穩定性。調整 temperature 會影響可重現性、品質與風格，因此應納入測試紀錄。

可採取行動：調整 temperature 後，應重新跑核心測試題，確認正確性、一致性與語氣沒有下降。
pm_notes.en: PMs should align temperature with the use case. For factual Q&A, summaries, or policy-based responses, use lower values and validate consistency with test cases before launch. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API Reference - Chat completions parameters
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Test data 是模型訓練完成後，用來評估最終效果的資料，理想上不應在訓練或調參過程中被模型或團隊反覆使用。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Test data 是模型訓練完成後，用來評估最終效果的資料，理想上不應在訓練或調參過程中被模型或團隊反覆使用。

若測試集太小、太乾淨或與真實使用差異大，會高估模型上線表現。
explanation.en:
Test data is a held-out dataset used to evaluate final model or system performance.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
測試資料應盡量模擬真實使用情境，並覆蓋常見問題、邊界案例、高風險案例與失敗情境。

可採取行動：確認 test data 沒有混入訓練資料，並覆蓋常見問題、邊界案例與高風險情境。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should keep test data separate from training data to avoid overly optimistic results.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary
Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer

summary.zh: Top-p 又稱 nucleus sampling，是控制模型隨機性的參數。
summary.en: A sampling parameter that limits generation to a cumulative probability mass of likely tokens.

explanation.zh:
Top-p 又稱 nucleus sampling，是控制模型隨機性的參數。模型只會從累積機率達到 p 的候選 token（語詞單位）中挑選下一個 token。

簡單理解：如果 top_p 設為 0.9，模型會只在最可能的一群候選答案中選，而不是從所有可能 token 中亂選。
explanation.en:
Top-p, also called nucleus sampling, is an inference setting that limits generation to a group of likely next tokens whose cumulative probability reaches a chosen threshold.

It is another way to control output variability. Lower values make the model choose from a narrower set of likely tokens, while higher values allow more diverse outputs.

Top-p is usually tuned carefully and should not be changed randomly together with temperature.

pm_notes.zh:
Temperature 和 top_p 都會影響生成多樣性，通常不建議同時大幅調整，否則難以判斷品質變化來源。

可採取行動：若調整 top_p，應一次只改一個生成參數並記錄效果，避免無法判斷品質變化來源。
pm_notes.en: PMs should avoid changing top-p and temperature at the same time without a clear reason. Tune generation settings only with defined quality goals and test cases. Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API Reference - Chat completions parameters
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Training 是讓模型從大量資料中學習規律的過程。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Training 是讓模型從大量資料中學習規律的過程。對大型模型來說，訓練成本非常高，通常需要大量 GPU（圖形處理器）、資料、工程與時間。

何時考慮訓練或微調：任務固定、有高品質資料、需要穩定格式或特定風格，而不是單純補充最新知識。
explanation.en:
Training is the process of adjusting model weights using data so the model learns patterns or behaviors.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 多數企業 GenAI 專案不會從零訓練大型模型，而是使用既有模型，再搭配 RAG、Prompt Engineering、Fine-tuning或 LoRA。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should distinguish training from inference, RAG, and prompt design. Each has different cost, risk, and timeline implications.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Training data 是用來讓模型學習的資料，會直接影響模型行為與能力。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Training data 是用來讓模型學習的資料，會直接影響模型行為與能力。

品質要求：正確、一致、覆蓋真實場景、沒有不該使用的敏感資料、格式適合模型學習、標註規則清楚。
explanation.en:
Training data is the data used to teach or fine-tune a model.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
若訓練資料本身有錯、過期、偏差或互相矛盾，模型會學到錯誤模式。資料準備與清理通常是訓練專案最大工作量之一。

可採取行動：在訓練前抽樣檢查資料錯誤、重複、過期與敏感資訊，並保留清理規則紀錄。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should verify data rights, quality, representativeness, and sensitive-data handling before using training data.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Hugging Face Datasets documentation
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Underfitting 是模型沒有充分學到資料中的規律，因此在訓練資料與測試資料表現都不好。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Underfitting 是模型沒有充分學到資料中的規律，因此在訓練資料與測試資料表現都不好。

可能原因：模型太小、訓練不足、特徵或資料不足、資料品質差、learning rate 或訓練設定不合適。
explanation.en:
Underfitting happens when a model has not learned enough from the data and performs poorly even on training examples.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh: 欠擬合代表目前方案能力不足或訓練策略不佳，可能需要更好資料、更合適模型、更長訓練或重新定義任務。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should consider underfitting when both test performance and training performance are weak.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Google ML Crash Course - Overfitting and underfitting
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

entry_metadata.change_note: 發布前中文內容可讀性修正

summary.zh: Validation data 是訓練過程中用來調整模型、選擇超參數與檢查效果的資料。
summary.en: A concept related to model training, fine-tuning, evaluation data, or inference-time behavior.

explanation.zh:
Validation data 是訓練過程中用來調整模型、選擇超參數與檢查效果的資料。

若 validation 表現好但 test 或真實場景表現差，可能代表資料切分不合理或過度調參。
explanation.en:
Validation data is used during development to tune settings and compare model variants.

This affects how a model is trained, evaluated, configured, or used during inference. Changes should be tested with representative examples because small parameter or data changes can produce noticeable behavior differences.

The impact should be validated with representative test cases, because training or inference changes can improve one workflow while weakening another.

pm_notes.zh:
驗證資料不能與訓練資料完全相同，否則無法判斷模型是否真的學會泛化。它通常用於比較不同 epoch、learning rate、batch size 或 prompt設計。

可採取行動：用 validation data 比較不同訓練設定，並避免把 validation 結果當成最終上線成績。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should make sure validation data reflects the product’s real tasks, not only easy examples.

Document the setting or data change and run test cases before promoting it to production.

primary_sources.title: Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary
Hugging Face Transformers - Trainer

summary.zh: Async API（應用程式介面）是非同步 API，系統送出任務後不必等待模型立即完成，可以稍後再取得結果。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Async API 是系統送出任務後，不必等待模型或後端流程立即完成，而是先取得任務 ID 或狀態，稍後再透過查詢、callback、webhook 或通知取得結果。

它適合長文件摘要、大量批次處理、報表生成、離線分析、需要排隊的高成本任務，或任何使用者不需要在同一個請求中立即拿到完整結果的流程。
explanation.en:
An async API lets a system submit work and retrieve the result later instead of waiting for completion in the same request.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
非同步可改善前台等待體驗與系統穩定性，但需要設計任務狀態、通知、查詢結果、失敗重跑、結果保存時間與使用者預期管理。

可採取行動：若改用非同步，需定義任務狀態、完成通知、錯誤訊息、使用者可查詢多久，以及任務失敗後是否允許重跑。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should use async patterns for long-running tasks such as batch summaries, document processing, or large exports.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

OpenAI API - Text generation

summary.zh: Autoscaling 是根據流量自動增加或減少運算資源。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Autoscaling 是根據流量自動增加或減少運算資源。

規劃時需搭配流量預測、預熱、容量保留、queue 與降級策略。
explanation.en:
Autoscaling automatically increases or decreases compute resources based on demand. It helps a service handle traffic spikes while reducing cost during quiet periods.

For AI systems, autoscaling may involve model servers, GPU capacity, queues, or supporting services such as retrieval and tool APIs. Scaling is not always instant, especially for large models.

Autoscaling needs to be tested under realistic load because cold starts, quota limits, and resource availability can affect response time.

pm_notes.zh:
自動擴縮可在尖峰時提高承載能力，在低流量時節省成本，但擴容通常需要時間，不一定能瞬間完成。大型模型也可能有冷啟動問題。

可採取行動：用壓力測試驗證擴容速度、尖峰承載與費用變化，避免流量突增造成等待或成本暴增。
pm_notes.en: PMs should define expected peak traffic and ask how quickly the system can scale. Load tests should verify whether autoscaling protects user experience without causing unexpected cost spikes. Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

Microsoft Learn - Azure OpenAI quotas and limits

summary.zh: Batch processing 是一次處理大量任務，例如批次摘要 1,000 份文件、批次產生 embedding（向量嵌入）或夜間產生報表。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Batch processing 是一次處理大量任務，例如批次摘要 1,000 份文件、批次產生 embedding（向量嵌入）或夜間產生報表。

管理重點：排程、失敗重跑、成本上限、rate limit（流量限制）、資料版本、處理進度與輸出品質抽查。
explanation.en:
Batch processing runs many tasks together, often offline or on a schedule.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
批次任務通常較重視 throughput與成本，不一定要求即時回覆。它適合離線處理、定期更新與大規模資料準備。

可採取行動：為批次任務定義處理截止時間、最大批量、失敗重跑與結果驗收方式。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should use batch processing when immediate user response is not required and throughput or cost efficiency matters more.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API - Batch API
Microsoft Learn - Azure OpenAI quotas and limits

summary.zh: Circuit breaker 是熔斷機制。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Circuit breaker 是熔斷機制。當某個服務持續失敗時，系統暫時停止呼叫該服務，以避免錯誤擴大或拖垮整體流程。

需要設計何時熔斷、多久恢復、如何告警與如何通知使用者。
explanation.en:
A circuit breaker stops calling a failing service temporarily so failures do not cascade through the system.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
這是正式上線系統常見的穩定性設計。當模型供應商、向量資料庫或工具 API（應用程式介面）異常時，熔斷可保護主系統，並觸發 fallback（備援處理）。

可採取行動：定義哪些錯誤率或 timeout條件會觸發熔斷，以及熔斷後要轉 fallback、排隊或暫停服務。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should include circuit breakers in production-readiness discussions for AI services that depend on external providers or tools.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

primary_sources.title: Microsoft Azure Architecture Center - Circuit Breaker pattern
Google SRE Book - Service Level Objectives

summary.zh: Cold start 是服務在閒置後重新啟動時，需要較長時間才能開始回應的現象。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Cold start 是服務在閒置後重新啟動時，需要較長時間才能開始回應的現象。

解法包括保持服務常駐、預熱、保留最小副本數、使用較小模型或將長任務改成非同步處理。
explanation.en:
Cold start is the extra delay when a service or model needs to start from an idle state.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
大型模型載入權重可能很慢，若使用者期待即時回覆，cold start 會嚴重影響體驗。

可採取行動：測量冷啟動時間，若影響體驗則討論預熱、常駐服務或最小實例數策略。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should test cold-start behavior for user-facing AI features. Warm-up or always-on capacity may be needed.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

Microsoft Learn - Azure OpenAI performance and latency

summary.zh: CPU 適合通用運算、API（應用程式介面）服務、資料前處理、排程任務與部分小模型推論。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
CPU 是通用運算處理器，適合處理應用服務、資料前處理、排程任務、API（應用程式介面）服務與部分小模型推論。

在 GenAI 架構中，CPU 常負責模型周邊工作，例如資料管線、檢索邏輯、權限檢查、請求路由與結果後處理。大型模型推論通常更依賴 GPU，但 CPU 資源仍會影響整體服務穩定性與吞吐量。
explanation.en:
A CPU is a general-purpose processor used for many types of computation.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh: CPU 彈性高，但處理大型深度學習模型通常不如 GPU 高效。若團隊規劃自建或私有部署模型服務，PM 應確認哪些工作跑 CPU、哪些需要 GPU，以及 CPU 是否會成為資料前處理、檢索或 API 服務的瓶頸。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should know that CPUs can support parts of the AI stack, but large model inference and training often need GPUs or accelerators.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

summary.zh: Endpoint 是應用程式呼叫模型服務的 API（應用程式介面）入口。
summary.en: The API address or service entry point used by applications to call a model.

explanation.zh:
Endpoint 是應用程式呼叫模型服務的 API（應用程式介面）入口。

Endpoint 管理牽涉資安、監控、SLA（服務水準協議）、流量限制與事故排查。
explanation.en:
An endpoint is the API address or service interface that an application calls to use a model or AI service.

Production systems often separate development, test, and production endpoints so experiments do not affect real users. Endpoints may also enforce authentication, rate limits, logging, and model version routing.

Endpoint design matters because downstream applications depend on its stability, response format, and availability.

pm_notes.zh:
正式環境通常會區分 dev、test、staging、prod endpoint（模型端點），避免測試流量影響正式服務。不同 endpoint 也可能使用不同模型版本、權限、配額或日誌設定。

可採取行動：確認 dev/test/staging/prod endpoint 分離，並記錄各環境模型版本、配額、權限與日誌設定。
pm_notes.en: PMs should confirm that dev, test, and production endpoints are separated and monitored. Endpoint changes should be reviewed because they can affect downstream systems, user experience, and launch stability. Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

summary.zh: GPU 是圖形處理器，因為適合大量平行運算，所以是 AI 訓練與推論常用硬體。
summary.en: A parallel computing processor commonly used to train and serve AI models.

explanation.zh:
GPU 是圖形處理器，因為適合大量平行運算，所以是 AI 訓練與推論常用硬體。

規劃時需考慮 GPU 型號、數量、VRAM（GPU 記憶體）、使用率、雲端費用、保留容量與擴縮策略。
explanation.en:
A GPU is a processor designed for highly parallel computation. In AI systems, GPUs are commonly used to train models and serve model inference because they can process many mathematical operations at once.

For self-hosted or private deployments, GPU type, memory, availability, and cost can become major constraints. Larger models typically require more GPU memory and may increase serving cost.

GPU planning affects capacity, latency, launch timeline, and whether the team can scale the service under load.

pm_notes.zh:
大型模型需要 GPU 才能有效訓練或推論。GPU 成本通常是自建模型服務的重要成本來源，也會影響可部署模型大小、throughput與 latency。

可採取行動：若採自建或私有部署，與技術團隊確認 GPU 型號、數量、利用率、成本與擴充策略。
pm_notes.en: PMs do not need to select GPU models directly, but they should understand how GPU availability affects cost, capacity, latency, and launch timelines. For private deployments, ask for capacity and scaling assumptions early. Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

name.zh: Agent 駕馭工程 / Agent Harness 工程

summary.zh: Harness Engineering 是為 AI agent 建立可靠執行環境、工具編排與安全限制的工程方法。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Harness Engineering 是圍繞 AI agent 建立可靠執行環境、限制機制、工具編排、上下文管理、狀態保存、錯誤恢復、驗證流程與安全護欄的工程方法。它的核心觀念是：agent 的表現不只取決於模型本身，也取決於模型外部的 harness。

可以把模型想成引擎，harness 是讓引擎能安全上路的車體、儀表板、煞車、導航、規則與維修系統。對 Coding Agent、長流程任務或可操作外部系統的 agent 來說，harness 會決定它是否能在真實環境中穩定完成任務。
explanation.en:
Harness engineering is the design of test harnesses, evaluation harnesses, and operational wrappers around AI systems. It helps teams run prompts, datasets, models, tools, and evaluations consistently.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
Harness Engineering 是新興但很實用的 agent 工程觀念。PM 應避免只問「用哪個模型」，也要確認 agent 外層是否有工具權限、流程控制、失敗處理、驗證機制與監控。

可採取行動：對可執行動作的 agent，要求專案文件描述工具清單、權限邊界、錯誤恢復、人工檢查點、日誌紀錄與驗收方式。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should treat the harness as part of product quality infrastructure. Without it, teams struggle to compare model versions, prompts, and agent behaviors reliably.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

primary_sources.title: Martin Fowler - Harness engineering for coding agent users
OpenAI API - Computer use harness patterns

summary.zh: Inference optimization 是讓模型推論更快、更省成本或更穩定的最佳化方法。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Inference optimization 是透過模型壓縮、快取、batching、硬體加速、量化、模型路由與 serving framework 讓模型回應更快、更省成本。

需注意：最佳化可能改變輸出品質，因此每次調整都應搭配回歸測試（用既有測試題重新確認結果沒有被改壞）與監控。
explanation.en:
Inference optimization improves how efficiently a model serves responses. Techniques may include batching, caching, quantization, routing, and hardware acceleration.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
當服務量變大時，推論最佳化通常比單純換更大模型更實際。它可改善 latency、throughput、GPU使用率與單次任務成本。

可採取行動：當成本或 latency 不達標時，先檢查快取、batching、量化、模型路由與輸出長度，再決定是否換模型。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should consider inference optimization when usage grows. It can improve latency and cost without changing the product experience.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

primary_sources.title: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM documentation
Hugging Face Optimum - Quantization

summary.zh: Load balancing 是將請求分散到不同服務節點、模型端點或區域，避免單一節點過載。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Load balancing 是將請求分散到不同服務節點、模型端點或區域，避免單一節點過載。

對 AI 服務而言，負載平衡需同時考慮模型版本一致性、資料地區限制、成本與 latency（延遲時間）。
explanation.en:
Load balancing distributes requests across multiple servers, model endpoints, or service instances so no single component becomes overloaded.

For AI systems, load balancing can improve availability and throughput, especially under peak traffic. It may also route requests across regions, model deployments, or fallback providers.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
當服務使用量增加時，負載平衡有助於提升穩定性、可用率與 throughput。若某個端點出現故障，也可將流量導向備援端點。

可採取行動：確認負載平衡策略是否能處理端點故障、區域異常與尖峰流量，並測試流量切換。
pm_notes.en: PMs should confirm load balancing behavior for production launches and peak events. If one endpoint fails or slows down, the product should have a clear routing or fallback strategy. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

primary_sources.title: Kubernetes - Services, Load Balancing, and Networking
Google SRE Book - Service Level Objectives

summary.zh: Model routing 是根據任務類型、成本、風險、語言、複雜度或容量，動態選擇不同模型。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Model routing 是根據任務類型、成本、風險、語言、複雜度或容量，動態選擇不同模型。

範例：簡單分類用便宜小模型；複雜推理用高階模型；敏感任務用內部部署模型；高峰時段改走備援模型。
explanation.en:
Model routing sends different tasks to different models based on cost, quality, latency, risk, or capability needs.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
模型路由可以降低成本並提升穩定性，但需要清楚的分流規則、品質監控與回歸測試，也就是用既有測試題重新確認不同模型分流後沒有影響關鍵任務品質，避免省成本卻犧牲重要任務品質。

可採取行動：定義哪些任務用小模型、哪些用高階模型，並持續監控分流後的成本與品質差異。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should define routing rules and quality expectations. Cheaper models may work for simple tasks, while complex tasks may need stronger models.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

primary_sources.title: Iterate.ai AI Glossary - Model Routing
Microsoft Learn - Azure OpenAI quotas and limits

summary.zh: Model size 通常與模型參數量、權重檔案大小與記憶體需求有關。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Model size 通常與模型參數量、權重檔案大小與記憶體需求有關。

選型時應根據任務複雜度、風險、成本、latency（延遲時間）與資料安全要求，而不是只追求最大模型。
explanation.en:
Model size usually refers to the number of parameters or the amount of memory needed to run a model.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh: 模型越大不一定越適合。大模型通常能力較強，但成本高、速度慢、部署需求高；小模型便宜快速，但可能無法處理複雜推理或專業任務。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should balance model size against latency, cost, quality, and deployment constraints.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM documentation

summary.zh: Parameters 是模型內部可學習的權重數量，例如 7B、13B、70B，其中 B 代表 billion，也就是十億。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Parameters 是模型內部可學習的權重數量，例如 7B、13B、70B，其中 B 代表 billion，也就是十億。

討論模型時要分清楚 parameters（模型內部權重數）與 inference parameters（temperature、top_p、max tokens 等設定）。
explanation.en:
Parameters are learned values inside a model. In deployment conversations, parameter count is often used to describe model scale.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh: 參數量越大通常代表模型容量越高，但也需要更多運算資源、VRAM與推論成本。參數量不是品質唯一指標，資料、訓練方式、指令調校與部署最佳化也很重要。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should treat parameter count as a rough indicator, not a product-quality guarantee.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

primary_sources.title: OpenAI API - Models
Google for Developers - Machine Learning Glossary

summary.zh: Quantization 是將模型權重用較低精度表示，例如從 16-bit 降到 8-bit 或 4-bit，以降低記憶體需求與推論成本。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Quantization 是將模型權重用較低精度表示，例如從 16-bit 降到 8-bit 或 4-bit，以降低記憶體需求與推論成本。

優點：降低硬體需求、加快推論速度、降低部署成本。風險：模型品質可能下降，尤其是複雜推理、特定語言或專業任務。
explanation.en:
Quantization reduces the numerical precision of model weights or activations to make models smaller or faster.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
量化是成本與效能最佳化常見手段，但不能只看速度與費用，必須用實際業務測試確認品質可接受。

可採取行動：量化後要用實際業務測試比較品質、latency與成本，確認效能提升沒有犧牲關鍵任務品質。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should validate quality after quantization. It can reduce cost and memory needs, but may affect output quality.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.

summary.zh: SLA 是 Service Level Agreement，對服務可用性、回應時間、支援與故障處理的正式承諾，通常是對外或跨單位承諾。
summary.en: A formal service commitment, often covering availability, support, and response expectations.

explanation.zh:
SLA 是 Service Level Agreement，對服務可用性、回應時間、支援與故障處理的正式承諾，通常是對外或跨單位承諾。

SLA 通常需要由 SLI（服務水準指標）指標與 SLO（服務水準目標）目標支撐。
explanation.en:
An SLA, or service level agreement, is a formal commitment about service availability, support, or performance. It may define uptime, response time, recovery time, or support obligations.

For AI products, SLA planning should account for model providers, internal services, retrieval systems, tool integrations, and fallback paths. A model API SLA alone does not guarantee the full product experience.

SLAs are most important when the AI capability becomes part of a production workflow or external-facing service.

pm_notes.zh:
AI 服務若進入正式營運流程，需要明確定義 SLA、備援機制、事故通報、供應商責任與不可用時的人工流程。

可採取行動：若 AI 支援正式流程，需定義可用率、故障通報、復原時間、供應商責任與人工備援流程。
pm_notes.en: PMs should confirm whether the AI feature needs an external SLA or only internal SLOs. If it supports production workflows, define failure communication, fallback behavior, and operational ownership. Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

primary_sources.title: AWS - What is a Service Level Agreement?
Google SRE Book - Service Level Objectives

summary.zh: SLI 是衡量服務品質的具體指標，例如延遲、可用性、錯誤率與成功率。
summary.en: A measurable service indicator used to evaluate whether an SLO is being met.

explanation.zh:
SLI 是 Service Level Indicator，用來衡量服務品質的具體指標，例如 latency（延遲時間）、availability、error rate、timeout（逾時） rate、success rate。

GenAI 服務除了傳統 SLI，也可加入 TTFT（首個 token 回應時間）、TTLT（最後 token 完成時間）、fallback（備援處理） rate、hallucination rate 與 groundedness（定錨程度）。
explanation.en:
An SLI, or service level indicator, is the metric used to measure whether a service is meeting its objectives. Common SLIs include availability, latency, error rate, throughput, and timeout rate.

SLIs make service quality observable. Without them, teams may rely on anecdotal feedback or demo behavior instead of production evidence.

For AI products, SLIs should be selected based on the user journey and the most likely failure modes.

pm_notes.zh:
SLI 是判斷 SLO是否達成的資料來源。若 SLI 定義不清，團隊無法客觀判斷服務品質。

可採取行動：選定可量測的 SLI，例如 P95 latency、error rate、availability，並確認 dashboard 能穩定取得資料。
pm_notes.en: PMs should align with the project team on which SLIs will be monitored after launch. The selected indicators should connect directly to user experience, operational risk, and support response. Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

primary_sources.title: Google SRE Book - Service Level Objectives
AWS - What is a Service Level Agreement?

summary.zh: SLO 是團隊為服務品質設定的目標，用來判斷系統是否達到預期水準。
summary.en: An internal target for service quality, such as latency, availability, or error rate.

explanation.zh:
SLO 是 Service Level Objective，團隊內部設定的服務品質目標，例如 P95 latency（第 95 百分位延遲）小於 5 秒、availability 高於 99.9%、error rate 小於 1%。

好的 SLO 應貼近使用者體驗與業務風險，而不只是工程容易量測的數字。
explanation.en:
An SLO, or service level objective, is an internal target for service reliability or performance, such as P95 latency under five seconds or error rate below one percent.

SLOs help teams align on what “good enough” means for a production service. They are usually measured using SLIs, such as latency, availability, or error rate.

For AI systems, SLOs should cover not only infrastructure uptime but also product-relevant behavior such as response time, failure rate, and sometimes quality indicators.

pm_notes.zh:
SLO 幫助產品與技術團隊對齊「什麼叫服務夠好」。它通常比 SLA更細，可用於日常監控與優化。

可採取行動：與技術團隊對齊內部 SLO，例如 P95 latency、錯誤率、可用率，並定義超標後的處理流程。
pm_notes.en: PMs should define SLOs that match user expectations and business criticality. For user-facing AI, include latency and error-rate targets, and decide what happens when the service falls below them. Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

summary.zh: Sync API（應用程式介面）是同步 API，系統送出請求後必須等待模型回覆完成，才繼續下一步。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
Sync API（應用程式介面）是同步 API，系統送出請求後必須等待模型回覆完成，才繼續下一步。

適合場景：即時問答、互動式聊天、前台使用者操作、需要立即回應的功能。
explanation.en:
A sync API waits for the result before returning a response to the caller.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
同步流程體驗直接受 latency影響，若任務很長或需要多工具呼叫，可能應改成 async API或背景批次處理。

可採取行動：若同步流程超過使用者可接受等待時間，應評估改為 async、streaming或拆成批次任務。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should use sync APIs for fast interactions and consider async APIs when tasks may take too long for a user to wait.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout.

OpenAI API - Batch API

summary.zh: VRAM 是 GPU（圖形處理器）上可用的記憶體。
summary.en: A concept related to deploying, scaling, operating, or optimizing AI services.

explanation.zh:
VRAM 是 GPU（圖形處理器）上可用的記憶體。模型越大、上下文越長、batch size 越大，通常需要越多 VRAM。

VRAM 也會影響同時服務請求數與成本效率。
explanation.en:
VRAM is memory available on a GPU. Large models need enough VRAM to load weights and process requests efficiently.

In production, this connects directly to reliability, scalability, cost, security, and launch readiness. It should be planned with realistic traffic assumptions and monitored after release.

This should be considered before production launch because infrastructure choices affect reliability, security, cost, and user experience under load.

pm_notes.zh:
能不能部署某個模型，常常取決於 GPU VRAM 是否足夠。若 VRAM 不足，可能需要量化、使用較小模型、分散部署或降低 batch size。

可採取行動：在選模型與硬體前確認 VRAM 需求，並評估量化、小模型或分散部署是否必要。
pm_notes.en:
PMs should understand VRAM as a deployment constraint for self-hosted or private model serving.

Confirm how the system behaves under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before production rollout. Confirm behavior under peak load, partial outage, and version changes before rollout.
